extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by versus against each entity: I counted only the Against Company, Against Promoters and Against Directors rows for criminal and statutory/regulatory proceedings, while excluding all By rows; this yields criminal 0 + 10 + 10 = 20 and regulatory 2 + 1 + 1 = 4. Tax proceedings are kept separate and, under the company-and-subsidiaries rule, include 11 against the Company and 0 against Subsidiaries; promoter/director tax rows are nil. The aggregate quantified amount against the Company and Subsidiaries is the Company-against amount of ₹386.76 million, with no quantified subsidiary amount, converted to ₹38.676 crore; promoter/director ₹17.74 million is excluded. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated consolidated Total of ₹1,697.17 million as at September 30, 2023, converted to ₹169.717 crore, rather than a re-sum. The concentration disclosure is company-wide consolidated revenue from operations for Fiscal 2023: top ten customers were 46.52%; no top-five customer percentage is disclosed. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted using 1 crore = ₹10 million.
